title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
At the George King Hotel, Mr. Bohns hosts a dinner in a private second-floor room overlooking the harbor square. His guests include Mrs. Maloway, Sir George, the talkative American Lethen and his clever daughter Yvonne, the preoccupied writer Llewellyn, the intelligent Janet Lathamington, and Mr. Bohns's former partner, Leopold.

During dinner, Yvonne asks Mr. Bohns about a large diamond he showed the group the previous night. Mr. Bohns identifies the diamond as his "Morning Star," a talisman he keeps in a special inner pocket. Yvonne challenges him to a bet, claiming she has a plan to steal it. They agree on a wager: if she succeeds, Mr. Bohns must provide six pairs of high-quality nylon stockings, while she bets a new cigarette case. The potential value of the stone is discussed, with Mr. Bohns noting it could be worth at least £30,000 even if re-cut.

To facilitate the demonstration, Mr. Bohns produces the diamond and passes it among the guests, including Madame Malo, Mr. Lechner, Llewellyn, Mr. Reuelin, and Leo Stein. Suddenly, Yvonne exclaims that the diamond has been dropped. As she reaches under the table to find it, a chaotic scene ensues; Sir George, Stein, Reuelin, Lady Ratherton, and Mrs. Maloway all bend down to assist her, causing a glass to fall. While the guests scramble in confusion, Mr. Bohns remains seated, watching with a cold smile while sipping his wine.
